scenarios.title: filters search results by category and price range @positive @e2e
scenarios.userAction: Navigate to /products, search for 'shoes', click the running-category filter, enter a $50–$150 price range and apply, then verify filtered product cards are visible and the result count contains 'found'.
expectedAssertions: at least one product card visible after search
at least one product card visible after category filter applied
result-count element contains text 'found' after category filter
at least one product card visible after price range filter applied

scenarios.title: clearing all filters returns to the unfiltered result set @edge @e2e
scenarios.userAction: Navigate to /products, search for 'jacket', record the initial product card count, apply the rain-category filter, then click 'clear all filters' and verify the count returns to its pre-filter value.
expectedAssertions: at least one product card visible after searching 'jacket'
product card count after clearing filters matches the pre-filter search count

locatorTable.notes: input tag confirms it is an input; search-bar class implies search purpose; no DOM evidence of type=search or role=searchbox. See Q1, pin 1.
button tag confirms role; accessible name guessed from class name. See Q2, pin 2.
No ARIA role evidence; common patterns are article or listitem but cannot promote without DOM proof. Reviewer: add data-testid=product-card or confirm ARIA role. See Q3, pin 3.
Element type entirely unknown — could be checkbox, link, or button. Accessible name guessed from class suffix. See Q4, pin 4.
No ARIA or semantic evidence. Kept as CSS fallback. Reviewer: add data-testid=result-count. See Q5, pin 5.
Label text unknown; assumes input is visually labeled. Could be getByPlaceholder if label absent. See Q6, pin 6.
Same uncertainty as .price-min. See Q6, pin 7.
button tag confirms role; accessible name guessed from class. See Q7, pin 8.

locatorTable.target: absorbed into positive assertion — locator dropped
locatorTable.notes: Conditional-logic block (KB-1.1.12) removed. Migrated spec asserts expect(arrayProductCards.first()).toBeVisible() directly. This locator is not emitted in Stage 2 output.
Same reasoning as .category-running. See Q4, pin 9.
button tag confirms role; accessible name guessed. See Q8, pin 10.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q1 unresolved: search input type/role not confirmed — assumed type=search or role=searchbox from class name semantics
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Add type=search to the HTML input, or provide the visible label text so getByLabel can be used, or confirm role=searchbox applies.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q2 unresolved: search button accessible name not confirmed — guessed from class suffix 'search-submit'
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Provide the exact visible text or aria-label of the button so the regex can be tightened. If icon-only, add aria-label=Search.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q3 unresolved: product card ARIA role unknown — kept CSS class as fallback rather than guessing article or listitem
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Confirm the element's ARIA role (article, listitem, or none). If confirmable, upgrade to getByRole. If not, add data-testid=product-card and switch to getByTestId.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q4 unresolved: category filter element type unknown — assumed button; could be checkbox or link
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Inspect the element: if input[type=checkbox], use getByRole('checkbox', { name: /running/i }) with .check(); if a[href], use getByRole('link', { name: /running/i }). Provide accessible name so regex can be verified.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q5 unresolved: result count element type unknown — kept CSS class; no ARIA role evidence
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Add data-testid=result-count to enable getByTestId, or confirm a role/aria-live attribute that allows a semantic upgrade.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q6 unresolved: price-min label/placeholder text unknown — assumed visible label containing 'min' or 'minimum price'
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Provide the exact label text or placeholder. If type=number, consider getByRole('spinbutton', { name: /min/i }) as alternative.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q6 unresolved: price-max label/placeholder text unknown — assumed visible label containing 'max' or 'maximum price'
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Same as pin 6 — provide exact label text or placeholder for the maximum price input.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q7 unresolved: apply-filters button accessible name not confirmed — guessed from class suffix 'apply-filters'
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Provide the button's visible text (e.g. 'Apply', 'Apply Filters', 'Filter') so the regex can be tightened or replaced with an exact match.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q4 unresolved (category-rain variant): same uncertainty as category-running — element type and accessible name both unconfirmed
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Same fallback logic as pin 4. Confirm element type and accessible name.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q8 unresolved: clear-filters button accessible name not confirmed — guessed from class suffix 'clear-filters'
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Provide the button's visible text (e.g. 'Clear', 'Clear all', 'Reset filters') so the regex can be tightened.
